$schema_note: M5 D5 drill bank. 6 pain statements drawn verbatim/paraphrased from §12 archetypes; each maps to one of 4 props (§13.1). Distractors included for solo-problem hint affordance.

pains.statement: Last quarter there was a $1,200 charge nobody could explain for two weeks.
We just hired 12 engineers — I'm Slack-pinging finance every time someone needs a card.
On €60K/mo of EUR-USD SaaS subscriptions we're leaking ~2.4% to FX spread.
Our bookkeeper spends 3–4 days every month re-keying supplier receipts into Xero.
I had to fire someone last year for expense fraud — I want to freeze a card from my phone if I see something weird.
Each of our 4 sites has its own manager — I need to cap weekly spend per site without calling the bank.

distractors.statement: I don't trust new fintechs with my money.
distractors.note: This is a trust objection (M2 family), not a prop-mappable pain.

distractors.statement: Our accountant won't deal with another tool.
distractors.note: Stakeholder-friction objection — the answer is to bring the bookkeeper onto the call (Class 5), not to pick a prop.

distractors.statement: We already have a corporate Visa from our bank.
distractors.note: Competitive-displacement objection (M2). Distractor in the D5 drill.

mapping_legend.exact_match: Pain → prop pairing is the §12 archetype's documented best-fit prop.
mapping_legend.partial_match: Pain could plausibly land Prop X but the §12 archetype's primary prop is Y — drill answer is Y.
